mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS regimes fall below 0.5% in > 40% of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors price in broader economic slowdown risks.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low HOUST regimes exceed 3% in > 50% of cases, or the predictive power of HOUST drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as higher discount rates weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent drop in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a supply-side shock combined with demand destruction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE fails to explain > 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces positive returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in consumer spending (RSAFS) following a recessionary dip drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days as corporate earnings recover and risk appetite improves.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in RSAFS recovery phases fall below 2% in > 60% of cases, or the predictive accuracy of RSAFS drops below 0.15 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% triggers wage inflation pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days as investors anticipate aggressive monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining oil prices (not in catalog) would signal strong supply-side growth, but since oil prices aren't available, we use INDPRO alone to predict positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high INDPRO regimes fall below 1% in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).
